Politics.headline: House Republicans shoot down bill that would pay federal airport workers
Politics.detail: House Republicans rejected a bill aimed at paying federal airport workers, exacerbating concerns about disruptions at airports.

Politics.headline: Fulton county accuses FBI of 'callous disregard' over ballot seizures
Politics.detail: Officials in Fulton County are urging a federal judge to order the return of more than 650 boxes of materials seized by FBI agents in January, claiming the FBI is showing 'callous disregard.'

Politics.headline: Canada fulfils 'ambitious promise,' reaching NATO's 2% defence spending target, Carney says
Politics.detail: Canada has reached NATO's 2% defence spending target, fulfilling what Carney describes as an 'ambitious promise'.

Politics.headline: Iran war rages, Strait of Hormuz still blocked as Trump pushes for a deal
Politics.detail: The Trump administration awaits Iran's response to its 15-point peace proposal as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked amid ongoing conflict.

Politics.headline: House GOP rejects DHS funding bill passed by Senate, drafts alternative
Politics.detail: The House of Representatives has rejected the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) funding bill approved by the Senate and is working on an alternative proposal.

Finance & Economy.headline: Financial markets fall to new 2026 low as oil rises again
Finance & Economy.detail: Financial markets have hit a new low for the year as the Iran war continues to drive anxiety among investors, businesses, and consumers, while rising gas prices are weighing down Americans' confidence in the economy. The S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow all finished the week lower, with the Dow entering correction territory.

Finance & Economy.headline: Dow Jones Industrial Average enters correction territory after five weeks of losses
Finance & Economy.detail: Stocks suffered a fifth straight weekly loss as oil prices climbed and mixed signals on Iran raised fears about inflation and growth. The Dow Jones Industrial Average has officially entered correction territory amidst these concerns.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks tumble to lowest close in 6 months as Iran war drags; Nasdaq confirms correction
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. stocks plunged Friday, hitting over six-month lows as the Middle East war suppressed risk appetite, with the Nasdaq confirming a correction. Rising oil prices fueled inflation fears and dampened rate cut expectations, leading markets to anticipate potential Fed rate hikes.

Finance & Economy.headline: Asda warns of fuel shortages as petrol prices continue to climb
Finance & Economy.detail: Asda has warned of temporary shortages at some petrol pumps as fuel prices continue to rise amidst the ongoing Iran war. This situation contributes to rising concerns about energy prices and their impact on the economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Malaysia moves to tighten rules for expats, raising fears of talent flight
Finance & Economy.detail: Malaysia's government plans to reduce the number of foreign workers to encourage local hiring and raise incomes. This move has raised concerns about a potential talent flight from the country, which could impact its economy.

Technology.headline: Judge Blocks Pentagon's Attempt to Blacklist Anthropic
Technology.detail: A federal judge has temporarily blocked the Pentagon from branding AI firm Anthropic a supply chain risk. The ruling is a setback for the Department of Defense's efforts to regulate AI development.

Technology.headline: Netflix Is Testing Game Chat
Technology.detail: Code within the Netflix Android app suggests the streaming giant is working on online chat features for its games. This indicates a move towards more multiplayer experiences on the platform.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: Microsoft is warning that AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping them automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating their attacks. This poses a significant threat to businesses and individuals alike.

Technology.headline: These RayNeo XR glasses effectively replaced my TV with HDR support - and they're on sale
Technology.detail: ZDNET reports on RayNeo XR glasses that provide an HDR viewing experience, effectively replacing a traditional TV, and are currently on sale. These glasses offer a portable and immersive entertainment solution.

Technology.headline: Yahoo turns to AI-powered answer engine Scout to lead it back to its roots in online search
Technology.detail: Yahoo is utilizing its AI-powered answer engine, Scout, hoping to revive its search capabilities. The company aims to leverage AI to provide users with more direct and informative answers.

Science.headline: Evacuated NASA astronaut says he couldn't speak in space. Why remains a mystery.
Science.detail: The astronaut who prompted NASA's first medical evacuation earlier this year said Friday that doctors still don't know why he suddenly fell sick at the International Space Station.

Science.headline: Millions of birds are being killed by outdoor cats.
Science.detail: Outdoor cats are responsible for the deaths of millions of birds, impacting various ecosystems.

Science.headline: High doses of antioxidants may impact future generations
Science.detail: A new study reveals that high doses of antioxidants may impact future generations, with male mice given common supplements like NAC producing offspring with subtle but significant facial and skull changes.

Science.headline: Scientists have developed a breakthrough “superfood” for honeybees
Science.detail: Scientists have developed a breakthrough “superfood” for honeybees by engineering yeast to produce the essential nutrients normally found in pollen; in controlled trials, colonies fed this superfood thrived.

Science.headline: Major volcanic eruptions might be driven by gas dissolving back into magma.
Science.detail: Research suggests that major volcanic eruptions could be triggered by gas dissolving back into magma, altering the pressure dynamics within the volcano.

Health.headline: Dietary supplement makers push the FDA to allow peptides and other new ingredients
Health.detail: Dietary supplement companies are advocating for the expansion of permissible ingredients in their products, urging the FDA to include peptides and other novel substances.

Health.headline: Global maternal deaths fell to 240,000 in 2023, but more than 100 countries still fall short of the global maternal mortality target as progress slows worldwide
Health.detail: A recent report indicates that while global maternal deaths have decreased to 240,000 in 2023, over 100 countries are still failing to meet the global maternal mortality target due to slowing progress.
Health.source: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation

Health.headline: This Popular Supplement May Increase Risk of Birth Defects, Study Finds
Health.detail: A study published on March 27, 2026, suggests that high doses of antioxidants, commonly perceived as harmless, may elevate the risk of birth defects, as demonstrated by facial and skull changes in offspring of male mice given supplements like NAC.

Health.headline: Pandemic Agreement Deadline Nears as Key Countries Put Equity at Center of Pathogen Sharing Negotiations
Health.detail: Negotiations for the Pandemic Agreement's Pathogen Access and Benefit-Sharing (PABS) system are intensifying, with key countries emphasizing equity.

Health.headline: EMA Backs Orodispersible Furosemide for Edema in Children
Health.detail: The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has supported the use of orodispersible furosemide for treating edema in children, offering a more convenient administration route.

Sports.headline: Tiger Woods arrested on suspicion of DUI after rollover crash in Florida
Sports.detail: Golfer Tiger Woods showed signs of impairment at the scene of a car crash Friday in which he struck another vehicle and rolled over, authorities said. He was arrested for DUI and property damage following the incident.

Sports.headline: Connecticut Sun reach deal to sell to Rockets owner, move team to Houston in 2027, AP source says
Sports.detail: The Connecticut Sun have reached an agreement to sell the team to Rockets owner Tilman Fertitta for $300 million and will move to Houston in 2027, according to a person familiar with the deal. This marks a significant shift in the landscape of women's professional basketball.

Sports.headline: Germany's Wirtz destroys Swiss and White caps England return as hero and villain
Sports.detail: Germany's Wirtz had a strong performance against Switzerland. White's return to the England team was marked by both heroic and villainous moments.

Sports.headline: Star-Spangled Blunder: Singer messes up national anthem on Opening Day at Citi Field
Sports.detail: A singer made errors while performing the national anthem on Opening Day at Citi Field. This led to some negative reactions from the crowd.

Sports.headline: BCCI Makes Major Rule Clarification On Catches Ahead Of IPL 2026
Sports.detail: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has announced a clarification to the rules regarding catches in cricket. This change is coming ahead of the 2026 Indian Premier League (IPL) season.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Swift Wins 7 Awards at iHeartRadio Music Awards 2026, Including Artist of the Year for 3rd Time in a Row
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Swift thanked her fiancé Travis Kelce for making her 'very happy and confident and free' as she accepted the Artist of the Year award at the iHeartRadio Music Awards.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars leaving Hollywood's Dolby Theatre and moving to L.A. Live
Entertainment.detail: The Academy Awards will be leaving the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood and moving to L.A. Live in 2029.

Entertainment.headline: Paul McCartney announces 18th solo album, The Boys of Dungeon Lane, promising introspection and revelation
Entertainment.detail: Paul McCartney has announced his 18th solo album titled 'The Boys of Dungeon Lane', which he says will be introspective and revealing.

Entertainment.headline: Tiger Woods charged with DUI after crash in Florida, authorities say
Entertainment.detail: Following a rollover car crash, Tiger Woods was charged with driving under the influence in Jupiter, Florida. In 2021, Woods was seriously injured in a rollover crash in Rolling Hills Estates, a Los Angeles suburb.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z has broken his silence regarding accusations in a 2024 sexual misconduct lawsuit tied to Sean 'Diddy' Combes in a new 'GQ' interview.

Crime & Justice.headline: Teenager in Wales handed life sentence after killing his mother with a hammer
Crime & Justice.detail: A teenager in Wales has been given a life sentence after being convicted of killing his mother with a hammer.

Crime & Justice.headline: Veteran, 83, allegedly shoved onto NYC subway tracks dies from injuries; illegal migrant charged with murder
Crime & Justice.detail: Richard Williams, an 83-year-old retired Air Force veteran, has died after allegedly being randomly shoved onto NYC subway tracks by an illegal immigrant, according to DHS reports, and the suspect has been charged with murder.

Crime & Justice.headline: Financial crime investigation culminates in arrests and frozen assets of high value
Crime & Justice.detail: Two individuals suspected of committing serious economic and financial crimes, leading to losses of tens of millions of euros, have been arrested during an international operation coordinated by Eurojust; the suspects are accused of bankruptcy fraud, embezzlement, and breach of accounting obligations.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man arrested on suspicion of officer assault and dangerous driving offences
Crime & Justice.detail: A 21-year-old man from Warrington has been arrested after he handed himself in for allegedly dragging an officer along the road on a motorbike while being detained. The man is suspected of Section 39 assault, assault of an emergency worker, and dangerous driving.

Crime & Justice.headline: Hove man jailed for series of sexual assaults
Crime & Justice.detail: Fattah Derrahi, a 59-year-old man from Hove, has been sentenced to six years in prison after admitting to sexually assaulting multiple women in public spaces over a four-year period. Derrahi would often compliment women's 'energy' before engineering physical situations to assault them.

Education.headline: Exhibit Showcases Excellence in Visual Art Education
Education.detail: The exhibition of award-winning student artwork recognizes and celebrates the high standard of visual art education in Queensland secondary schools, featuring 40 outstanding artworks from March 28 to September 13.

Education.headline: Italy sees widespread strikes across transport, schools, media
Education.detail: Workers in Italy participated in a coordinated strike impacting public transport, education, and media, due to labor disputes, with teachers and school staff calling for secure employment and changes in principal selection, leading to school closures in many areas.

Education.headline: JI launches education initiative for underprivileged segments
Education.detail: Jamaat-e-Islami (JI) has initiated an education campaign to support underprivileged children in Karachi, providing them with books, stationery, and other educational materials, in response to the alarming education crisis in Sindh.

Education.headline: UJ to host inaugural neurodiversity career fair in Soweto
Education.detail: The University of Johannesburg's (UJ) Centre for Neurodiversity will host its first career fair dedicated to neurodiversity at the Soweto Campus on March 28, offering opportunities for neurodivergent individuals to connect with industry and educational partners.

Education.headline: Delhi School Annual Result 2026: DoE Likely To Release Class 3, 4, 5 Scorecards Today, Download Link Here
Education.detail: The Delhi Department of Education (DoE) is expected to release the scorecards for classes 3, 4, and 5 today, providing students and parents with access to their annual results.

Lifestyle.headline: Restaurants may be quietly inflating your tip — and diners are starting to notice
Lifestyle.detail: Some restaurants may be quietly inflating tip amounts, leading to increased scrutiny from diners who are starting to notice discrepancies in their bills.

Lifestyle.headline: Couple ditches corporate grind as cost-of-living crisis leads to decade-long van travel.
Lifestyle.detail: A couple decided to leave their corporate jobs due to the rising cost of living and have been traveling in a van for a decade.

Lifestyle.headline: From nolo to blotto: six cocktails for spring – recipes
Lifestyle.detail: A selection of six cocktail recipes perfect for spring, ranging from non-alcoholic to alcoholic options.

Lifestyle.headline: Transformability, heritage and bold colour define 2026 high jewellery
Lifestyle.detail: Eight jewelry houses reveal their high jewelry designs for 2026, featuring transformable pieces, the return of grand feu enamel, and the prominence of Paraiba tourmalines.

Lifestyle.headline: Why your sugar cravings won't go away, even if you stop eating sweets.
Lifestyle.detail: An explainer on why sugar cravings persist even after eliminating sweets from your diet, exploring the underlying causes and potential solutions.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Chinese anxiety about Trump visit
Opinion/Editorial.detail: As Chinese President Xi Jinping prepares for a high-stakes summit with US President Donald Trump, the traditional power dynamics of the China-Japan-US triangle have been destabilized by the diplomatic success of Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi in Washington, leading to anxiety in Beijing.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Protect Mandarin instructors' rights
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Mandarin-language instructors from National Taiwan Normal University's (NTNU) Mandarin Training Center protested, demanding better labor rights and protections, with a focus on ending three-month contracts.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Annual insanity
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discussing current events in Indonesia.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Biggest hour for Earth
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The world observes the 20th Earth Hour today amid a fuel crisis sparked by human conflict, making the switch to renewable energy faster and irreversible.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: 'Strait of Trump': US president says Iran must open key waterway
Opinion/Editorial.detail: US President Donald Trump insisted Iran must open up the Strait of Hormuz to oil traffic to make a peace deal and dubbed the crucial waterway the 'Strait of Trump.'

World.headline: Iranian Red Crescent says more than 92,600 civilian units were damaged across the country in US-Israeli attacks
World.detail: The Iranian Red Crescent reported extensive damage to civilian infrastructure due to the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran.

World.headline: Houthis warn 'fingers on the trigger' as US-Israel war on Iran continues
World.detail: The Houthis have issued a warning amidst the ongoing conflict, signaling their continued readiness for engagement.

World.headline: Israel launches strikes on nuclear sites as Iran warns of retaliation
World.detail: Amid escalating tensions, Israel has launched attacks on nuclear facilities within Iran, prompting strong warnings of retaliation from Tehran.

World.headline: European powers accuse Russia of helping Iran target US forces in Middle East
World.detail: European nations are alleging that Russia is providing assistance to Iran in targeting US forces in the Middle East, further complicating the geopolitical landscape.

World.headline: Iran's missiles pierce Israel's defenses, raising doubts about interceptors
World.detail: Recent strikes have exposed vulnerabilities in Israel's defense systems, as Iranian missiles have successfully penetrated them, raising concerns about the effectiveness of interceptor technologies.

Zimbabwe.headline: No solution, no school – Teachers threaten job action over poor salaries
Zimbabwe.detail: Teachers are threatening job action due to poor salaries, indicating potential disruptions in the education sector if their demands are not addressed.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZWG annual inflation rises to 4,4% as Middle East tension drive costs
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe's annual inflation has risen to 4.4%, reportedly influenced by the ongoing tensions in the Middle East which are driving up costs.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe legalises ban on second-hand clothes imports
Zimbabwe.detail: The government has formalized the ban on the import of second-hand clothes, a move with potential implications for the textile industry and consumers.

Zimbabwe.headline: Government extends ZIMSEC examination fees payment deadline to May 22
Zimbabwe.detail: The deadline for payment of ZIMSEC examination fees has been extended to May 22, providing relief to students and parents.

Zimbabwe.headline: Eleven nurses arrested, fined over protest at Marondera Hospital
Zimbabwe.detail: Eleven nurses were arrested and fined for protesting at Marondera Hospital, highlighting ongoing tensions and grievances within the healthcare sector.
